options.name: Agonizing Blast
options.description: Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage. You can add your Charisma modifier to that spell’s damage rolls.

options.name: Armor of Shadows
options.description: You can cast Mage Armor on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Ascendant Step
options.description: You can cast Levitate on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Devil’s Sight
options.description: You can see normally in Darkness—both magical and nonmagical—to a distance of 120 feet.

options.name: Eldritch Mind
options.description: You have Advantage on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain Concentration.

options.name: Eldritch Smite
options.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can expend a Pact Magic spell slot to deal an extra 1d8 Force damage to the target, plus another 1d8 per level of the spell slot, and you can give the target the Prone condition if it is Huge or smaller.

options.name: Eldritch Spear
options.description: Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage and has a range of 10 feet or more. When you cast that cantrip, its range increases by a number of feet equal to 30 times your Warlock level.

options.name: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
options.description: You can read all writing.

options.name: Fiendish Vigor
options.description: You can cast False Life on yourself without expending a spell slot. When you cast it this way, you don’t roll the die for the Temporary Hit Points; instead, you automatically get the highest number on the die.

options.name: Gaze of Two Minds
options.description: You can use a Bonus Action to touch a willing creature and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. The connection ends if you don’t maintain it or if the distance between you and the creature exceeds 1 mile. While perceiving through the other creature’s senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are Blinded and Deafened to your own surroundings.

options.name: Gift of the Depths
options.description: You can breathe underwater, and you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed. You can also cast Water Breathing once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a Long Rest.

options.name: Gift of the Protectors
options.description: A new page appears in your Book of Shadows when you gain this invocation. With your permission, a creature can use its action to write its name on that page. Whenever any creature whose name is on the page is reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright, the creature magically drops to 1 Hit Point instead. Once this magic is triggered, no creature can benefit from it until you finish a Long Rest. Magic can add no more than eight names to the page.

options.name: Investment of the Chain Master
options.description: When you cast Find Familiar, you infuse the summoned familiar with a measure of your eldritch power, granting the creature the following benefits: The familiar gains either a Fly Speed or Swim Speed (your choice) of 40 feet. As a Bonus Action, you can command the familiar to take the Attack action. The familiar’s weapon attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming Immunity and Resistance to nonmagical attacks. If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC. When the familiar takes damage, you can use your Reaction to grant it Resistance against that damage.

options.name: Lessons of the First Ones
options.description: You have received knowledge from an elder entity of the multiverse, allowing you to gain one Origin feat of your choice. You can select this invocation more than once, gaining a new Origin feat each time.

options.name: Lifedrinker
options.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage (your choice) to the creature.

options.name: Maddening Hex
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you cause a psychic disturbance around the target cursed by your Hex spell or by a Warlock feature of yours, such as Sign of Ill Omen and Fiendish Vigor. When you do so, you deal Psychic damage to the cursed target and each creature of your choice within 5 feet of it. The Psychic damage equals your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 damage). To use this invocation, you must be able to see the cursed target, and it must be within 30 feet of you.

options.name: Mask of Many Faces
options.description: You can cast Disguise Self without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Master of Myriad Forms
options.description: You can cast Alter Self without expending a spell slot.

options.name: One with Shadows
options.description: When you are in an area of Dim Light or Darkness, you can cast Invisibility on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Otherworldly Leap
options.description: You can cast Jump on yourself without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Pact of the Blade
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can conjure a pact weapon in your hand—a Simple or Martial melee weapon of your choice with which you bond—or create a bond with a magic weapon you touch; you can’t bond with a magic weapon if someone else is attuned to it or another Warlock is bonded with it. Until the bond ends, you have proficiency with the weapon, and you can use it as a Spellcasting Focus. Whenever you attack with the bonded weapon, you can use your Charisma modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity; and you can cause the weapon to deal Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage or its normal damage type. Your bond with the weapon ends if you use this feature’s Bonus Action again, if the weapon is more than 5 feet away from you for 1 minute or more, or if you die. A conjured weapon disappears when the bond ends.

options.name: Pact of the Chain
options.description: You learn the Find Familiar spell and can cast it as a Magic action without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell, you choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: Imp, Pseudodragon, Quasit, Skeleton, Slaad Tadpole, Sphinx of Wonder, Sprite, or Venomous Snake. Additionally, when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own with its Reaction.

options.name: Pact of the Tome
options.description: Stitching together strands of shadow, you conjure forth a book in your hand at the end of a Short or Long Rest. This Book of Shadows contains eldritch magic that only you can access, granting you the following benefits. Cantrips and Rituals. When the book appears, choose three cantrips, and choose two level 1 spells that have the Ritual tag. The cantrips can be from any class’s spell list, and they are Warlock spells for you while they’re in the book. The spells can be from any class’s spell list, and you can cast them as Rituals while they’re in your book; you can’t cast them as non-Ritual spells. On your adventures, you can add other Ritual spells to your Book of Shadows.

options.name: Repelling Blast
options.description: Choose one of your known Warlock cantrips that deals damage. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with that cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from you.

options.name: Thirsting Blade
options.description: When you take the Attack action, you can attack twice with your pact weapon instead of once. You can use this invocation twice, and you recover all expended uses when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

options.name: Undying Servitude
options.description: You can cast Animate Dead without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.name: Visions of Distant Realms
options.description: You can cast Arcane Eye without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Voice of the Chain Master
options.description: You can communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar’s senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Additionally, while perceiving through your familiar’s senses, you can also speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

options.name: Whispers of the Grave
options.description: You can cast Speak with Dead without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Witch Sight
options.description: You can see the true form of any Shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic while the creature is within 30 feet of you and within your line of sight.

options.name: Alchemy Jug
options.description: No attunement required. An alchemy jug can produce various liquids on command.

options.name: Bag of Holding
options.description: No attunement required. This bag has an interior space considerably larger than its outside dimensions.

options.name: Cap of Water Breathing
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this cap underwater, you can breathe normally.

options.name: Common magic item (not a Potion, Scroll, or cursed)
options.description: Varies. You can select this option multiple times, choosing a different common magic item each time.

options.name: Goggles of Night
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing these dark lenses, you have Darkvision out to 60 feet.

options.name: Manifold Tool
options.description: Requires attunement. A tool that can transform into different types of artisan’s tools.

options.name: Repeating Shot
options.description: Requires attunement. A weapon with the Ammunition property that magically produces one piece of ammunition each time you make a ranged attack.

options.name: Returning Weapon
options.description: No attunement required. A thrown weapon with the Thrown property that returns to your hand after each throw.

options.name: Rope of Climbing
options.description: No attunement required. This 60-foot length of rope can animate on command.

options.name: Sending Stones
options.description: No attunement required. A matched pair of stones allows telepathic communication between the bearers.

options.name: Shield +1
options.description: No attunement required. While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC.

options.name: Wand of Magic Detection
options.description: No attunement required. This wand has 3 charges and can cast Detect Magic.

options.name: Wand of Secrets
options.description: No attunement required. This wand has 3 charges and can detect secret doors and traps.

options.name: Wand of the War Mage +1
options.description: Requires attunement by a spellcaster. While holding this wand, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls.

options.name: Weapon +1
options.description: No attunement required. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.

options.name: Wraps of Unarmed Power +1
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing these wraps, your Unarmed Strikes deal +1 damage.

options.name: Armor +1
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this armor, you have a +1 bonus to AC.

options.name: Boots of Elvenkind
options.description: No attunement required. While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound.

options.name: Boots of the Winding Path
options.description: Requires attunement. While attuned, you can teleport up to 15 feet to a space you can see.

options.name: Cloak of Elvenkind
options.description: Requires attunement. While attuned and the hood is up, you have Advantage on Stealth checks.

options.name: Cloak of the Manta Ray
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this cloak, you can breathe underwater and have a Swim Speed of 60 feet.

options.name: Dazzling Weapon
options.description: Requires attunement. When you score a critical hit, the target is Blinded until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Eyes of Charming
options.description: Requires attunement. This pair of crystal lenses fits over the eyes. While wearing them, you can cast Charm Person (save DC 13) without expending a spell slot.

options.name: Eyes of Minute Seeing
options.description: No attunement required. These crystal lenses grant you Advantage on Investigation checks made to examine an object or area within 1 foot.

options.name: Gloves of Thievery
options.description: No attunement required. These gloves give you a +5 bonus to Sleight of Hand checks and Dexterity checks made to pick locks.

options.name: Helm of Awareness
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this helmet, you can’t be surprised and have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

options.name: Lantern of Revealing
options.description: No attunement required. While lit, this lantern sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and reveals Invisible creatures and objects.

options.name: Mind Sharpener
options.description: Requires attunement. This item can interrupt a failed Concentration check, allowing you to maintain concentration.

options.name: Necklace of Adaption
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment, and you have Advantage on saving throws against harmful gases.

options.name: Pipes of Haunting
options.description: No attunement required. You must be proficient with wind instruments to use these pipes. They have 3 charges and can cast Fear.

options.name: Repulsion Shield
options.description: No attunement required. A shield that can push creatures away when you perform the Shove action.

options.name: Ring of Swimming
options.description: No attunement required. You have a Swim Speed of 40 feet while wearing this ring.

options.name: Ring of Water Walking
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this ring, you can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

options.name: Sentinel Shield
options.description: No attunement required. While holding this shield, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Perception checks.

options.name: Spell-Refueling Ring
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this ring, once per day when you expend a spell slot, you can regain one expended spell slot of the same level or lower.

options.name: Wand of Magic Missiles
options.description: No attunement required. This wand has 7 charges and can cast Magic Missile.

options.name: Wand of Web
options.description: Requires attunement by a spellcaster. This wand has 7 charges and can cast Web.

options.name: Weapon of Warning
options.description: Requires attunement. This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

options.name: Armor of Resistance
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this armor, you have Resistance to one type of damage.

options.name: Dagger of Venom
options.description: No attunement required. This dagger has a groove running down the blade. You can use a Bonus Action to cause thick, dark poison to coat the blade.

options.name: Elven Chain
options.description: No attunement required. You gain a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armor. You are considered proficient with this armor even if you lack proficiency with medium armor.

options.name: Ring of Feather Falling
options.description: Requires attunement. When you fall while wearing this ring, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

options.name: Ring of Jumping
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this ring, you can cast Jump on yourself as a Bonus Action.

options.name: Ring of Mind Shielding
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine if you are lying, or know your alignment.

options.name: Shield +2
options.description: No attunement required. While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC.

options.name: Uncommon Wondrous Item (not cursed)
options.description: Varies. You can select this option multiple times, choosing a different uncommon wondrous item each time.

options.name: Wand of the War Mage +2
options.description: Requires attunement by a spellcaster. While holding this wand, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

options.name: Weapon +2
options.description: No attunement required. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.

options.name: Wraps of Unarmed Power +2
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing these wraps, your Unarmed Strikes deal +2 damage.

options.name: Armor +2
options.description: No attunement required. While wearing this armor, you have a +2 bonus to AC.

options.name: Arrow-Catching Shield
options.description: Requires attunement. You gain a +2 bonus to AC against ranged attacks while holding this shield.

options.name: Flame Tongue
options.description: Requires attunement. While the blade is ignited, the sword deals an extra 2d6 Fire damage on a hit and sheds Bright Light in a 40-foot radius.

options.name: Rare Wondrous Item (not cursed)
options.description: Varies. You can select this option multiple times, choosing a different rare wondrous item each time.

options.name: Ring of Free Action
options.description: Requires attunement. While wearing this ring, Difficult Terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement, and magic can neither reduce your Speed nor cause you to have the Paralyzed or Restrained condition.

options.name: Ring of Protection
options.description: Requires attunement. You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws while wearing this ring.

options.name: Ring of the Ram
options.description: Requires attunement. This ring has 3 charges and can cast a force ram attack.

options.name: Ambush
options.description: When you make a Dexterity (Stealth) check or an Initiative roll, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the roll, unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

options.name: Bait and Switch
options.description: When you’re within 5 feet of a creature on your turn, you can expend one Superiority Die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 5 feet of movement and the creature is willing and doesn’t have the Incapacitated condition. This movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks. Roll the Superiority Die. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.

options.name: Commander’s Strike
options.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks to direct one of your companions to strike. When you do so, choose a willing creature who can see or hear you and expend one Superiority Die. That creature can immediately use its Reaction to make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, adding the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll on a hit.

options.name: Commanding Presence
options.description: When you make a Charisma (Intimidation, Performance, or Persuasion) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add that die to the roll.

options.name: Disarming Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to disarm the target. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or drop one object of your choice that it’s holding, with the object landing in its space.

options.name: Distracting Strike
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to distract the target. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack’s damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has Advantage if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.

options.name: Evasive Footwork
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die and take the Disengage action. You also roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Feinting Attack
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die to feint, choosing one creature within 5 feet of yourself as your target. You have Advantage on your next attack roll against that target this turn. If that attack hits, add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll.

options.name: Goading Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to goad the target into attacking you. Add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Lunging Attack
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die and take the Dash action. If you move at least 5 feet in a straight line immediately before hitting with a melee attack as part of the Attack action on this turn, you can add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll.

options.name: Maneuvering Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to maneuver one of your comrades into another position. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack’s damage roll, and choose a willing creature who can see or hear you. That creature can use its Reaction to move up to half its Speed without provoking an Opportunity Attacks from the target of your attack.

options.name: Menacing Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to frighten the target. Add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Parry
options.description: When another creature damages you with a melee attack roll, you can take a Reaction and expend one Superiority Die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your Superiority Die plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).

options.name: Precision Attack
options.description: When you miss with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die, roll that die, and add it to the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit.

options.name: Pushing Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to drive the target back. Add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet directly away from you.

options.name: Rally
options.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Superiority Die to bolster the resolve of a companion. Choose an ally of yours within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Superiority Die roll plus half your Fighter level (round down).

options.name: Riposte
options.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack roll, you can take a Reaction and expend one Superiority Die to make a melee attack roll with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against the creature. If you hit, add the Superiority Die to the attack’s damage.

options.name: Sweeping Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to damage another creature. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on your Superiority Die. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

options.name: Tactical Assessment
options.description: When you make an Intelligence (History or Investigation) check or a Wisdom (Insight) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add that die to the ability check.

options.name: Trip Attack
options.description: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the attack’s damage roll. If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the Prone condition.

options.name: Careful Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, spend 1 Sorcery Point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

options.name: Distant Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 5 feet, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double the spell's range. When you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to make the range of the spell 30 feet.

options.name: Empowered Spell
options.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls. You can use Empowered Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

options.name: Extended Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.

options.name: Heightened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to give one target of the spell Disadvantage on its first saving throw against the spell.

options.name: Quickened Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that has a casting time of an action, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to change the casting time to a Bonus Action for this casting.

options.name: Seeking Spell
options.description: If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll. You can use Seeking Spell even if you have already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.

options.name: Subtle Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to cast it without any Verbal, Somatic, or Material components, except Material components that are consumed by the spell or that have a cost specified in the spell.

options.name: Transmuted Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that deals a type of damage from the following list, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Thunder.

options.name: Twinned Spell
options.description: When you cast a spell that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to cast the spell at its lowest level and choose a second target in range, also without using a higher slot.

options.name: Bat
options.description: You know the Dancing Lights cantrip. You also gain Blindsight with a range of 10 feet.

options.name: Butterfly
options.description: You know the Light cantrip. When you make a High Jump, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine how high you can jump.

options.name: Crane
options.description: You know the Guidance cantrip. When you miss a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

options.name: Horse
options.description: You know the Message cantrip. When you expend 1 Focus Point to use Step of the Wind, your Speed increases by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Tortoise
options.description: You know the Spare the Dying cantrip. When you expend 1 Focus Point to use Patient Defense, you have a +1 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Comet
options.description: When you take the Search action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Wisdom check.

options.name: Eclipse
options.description: When you take the Hide action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Dexterity (Stealth) check.

options.name: Sunburst
options.description: When you take the Study action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Intelligence check.

options.name: Sea Storm
options.description: You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Cold, Lightning, or Thunder. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can change this choice.

options.name: Volcano
options.description: You gain Resistance to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Fire, or Poison. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can change this choice.

options.name: Beholder
options.description: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed for 10 minutes (you can hover). Additionally, as a Magic action you can expend 1 Focus Point to fire four rays from your eyes at one or more targets within 120 feet; make a ranged spell attack for each. On a hit, each ray deals Force damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.

options.name: Chromatic Dragon
options.description: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to replace one of your attacks with a 30-foot Cone exhalation. Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save it takes damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier; on success, half as much.

options.name: Displacer Beast
options.description: When you expend a Focus Point to use Flurry of Blows or Step of the Wind, you can expend 1 additional Focus Point to cast Mirror Image as part of that Bonus Action.

options.name: Troll
options.description: At the start of each of your turns, you regain Hit Points equal to 5 plus your Wisdom modifier if you are Bloodied and have at least 1 Hit Point. Any severed body part regrows after you finish a Short or Long Rest.

options.name: Colossus Slayer
options.description: Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn.

options.name: Horde Breaker
options.description: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

options.name: Escape the Horde
options.description: Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you.

options.name: Multiattack Defense
options.description: When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

options.name: Owl
options.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

options.name: Panther
options.description: You have a Climb Speed equal to your Speed.

options.name: Salmon
options.description: You have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

options.name: Banishing Shot
options.description: Your ammunition temporarily sequesters your target in a harmless demiplane. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. While banished, the creature has the Incapacitated condition and a Speed of 0. At the end of its next turn, the target reappears in the space it left or, if that space is occupied, in the nearest unoccupied space.

options.name: Beguiling Shot
options.description: Your ammunition beguiles your target. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition until the start of your next turn, treating either you or one of your allies within 30 feet of the target (your choice) as the charmer. The Charmed condition ends early if the charmer attacks the target, deals damage to it, or forces it to make a saving throw.

options.name: Bursting Shot
options.description: You imbue your ammunition with explosive force energy. Immediately after you deal damage to the creature, your target and each creature within a 10-foot Emanation originating from the target take Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die.

options.name: Enfeebling Shot
options.description: Your ammunition saps your target’s strength. The creature you hit takes additional Necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn. Whenever a target Poisoned in this way hits with an attack roll, it subtracts an amount equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die from the total damage of that attack.

options.name: Grasping Shot
options.description: Your ammunition creates clutching brambles around your target. The creature you hit takes additional Slashing damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the Restrained condition for 1 minute or until you use this option again. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your Arcane Shot DC, removing the brambles and ending the Restrained condition on the target on a successful check.

options.name: Piercing Shot
options.description: You give your ammunition an ethereal quality. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, the ammunition shoots forward in a 30-foot Line that is 1 foot wide, originating from you, then vanishes. The Line ignores cover, as the ammunition phases through solid objects. Each creature in the Line must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit plus additional Piercing damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

options.name: Seeking Shot
options.description: Your ammunition can seek out a target. When you use this option, you don’t make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, choose one creature you have seen in the last minute. The ammunition flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover. If the target is within your weapon’s long range, the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes damage as if it were hit plus additional Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die, and you learn the target’s current location. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. If the target is beyond your weapon’s long range, the ammunition disappears after traveling as far as it can.

options.name: Shadow Shot
options.description: Your ammunition occludes your foe’s vision with shadows. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Blinded condition until the end of its next turn.
